description: Real journal article; a diacritic (o-circumflex) in an author name and a colon in the title exercise Unicode and punctuation across every format.

record.title: Automatic diagnosis of the 12-lead ECG using a deep neural network

description: Synthetic: literal brace characters and XML-special characters in the title. RIS, JATS, and CSL-JSON preserve the braces verbatim; BibTeX consumes them as capitalization grouping, which is the documented per-case loss.

record.title: The {ECG} Benchmark & <Deep> Learning: a Möbius Review
record.abstract: An abstract containing <tags>, & ampersands, and {braces} to stress escaping.

description: Synthetic: an organization (literal) author with no comma. RIS carries it via the no-comma-multiword heuristic; JATS carries it as <collab>; BibTeX braces it.

record.title: Global status report on noncommunicable diseases

description: Synthetic: an entry with no DOI at all, so the record identity comes from its own fields. RIS carries the caller-supplied id via the ID tag; JATS regenerates it (documented loss).

record.title: A No-DOI Conference Paper for the Round-Trip Suite

description: The single sanctioned synthetic fake (D8), reused from the citation-audit example. Its DOI must KEEP failing to resolve; it is here so the round-trip suite exercises the expect-unresolvable marker path without any network resolution.

record.title: Contrastive masked autoencoders for single-lead ECG anomaly detection
record.note: (synthetic, for demonstration); this reference does not exist

description: A real DataCite (Dryad) dataset DOI (D22). Type 'dataset' round-trips through RIS (DATA) and JATS (data); BibTeX collapses it to 'document' (documented loss). Non-DOI metadata is illustrative.

description: A real DataCite (Zenodo) software DOI (D22) carrying a version string. Type 'software' and the version round-trip through JATS; RIS drops the version and BibTeX drops both the version and the type (documented losses). Non-DOI metadata is illustrative.
